# UC-11.1.14 · Exchange Client Access RPC Averaged Latency (Perfmon)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Performance, Availability &middot; **Status:** Draft

*We watch Outlook and mailbox response times, so slow mail clients point to a host or storage issue we can fix early.*

---

## Description

RPC averaged latency on Client Access servers reflects MAPI/HTTP and Outlook responsiveness; sustained elevation above Microsoft guidance usually indicates CPU, storage, or network contention before users flood the service desk.

## Value

Provides an early warning for mailbox connectivity pain separate from transport queue depth, helping teams split client-access incidents from mail-flow incidents quickly.

## Implementation

Deploy `perfmon` inputs on all Exchange servers running the MSExchange RpcClientAccess process. Confirm object/counter spelling matches the OS locale. Baseline per site; some organizations alert >200 ms. Correlate with database failover events and storage latency.

## SPL

```spl
index=perfmon object="MSExchange RpcClientAccess" counter="RPC Averaged Latency" earliest=-4h
| eval ms=coalesce(Value, value)
| where ms>250
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(ms) as avg_rpc_ms max(ms) as peak_rpc_ms by _time, host
| sort -peak_rpc_ms
```

## Visualization

Timechart of avg vs peak latency, table (host × peak), single value (worst CAS).

## Known False Positives

Latency during storage firmware upgrades, antivirus full scans, or maintenance windows on CAS role.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)
